harness: criterion 10-sample median, --runs implicit (criterion warm-up + sustained sampling)
findings: Software tile_mma_v1 BEATS production marlin_gemm at M in [1, 8]. Crossover at M ~= 16. Above M = 16 the production dequant+matmul saturates at ~1.4 ms while software scales linearly and loses badly.

Why software wins at low M: marlin_gemm at M <= 16 dispatches to awq_gemv_int4_kt_bf16 which has kt_mloop shmem setup + dispatch overhead. The naive software per-cell dot product has none of that — at M = 1 there are 11008 cells with K = 4096 reads each, fully memory-bound, pure throughput.

Why production wins at high M: at M >= 32 marlin_gemm uses the dequant + cuBLAS GEMM fallback. cuBLAS BF16 GEMM is heavily tuned and runs roughly constant ~1.4 ms across M = 16..256 (saturated). Software remains O(M*N*K/threads) and loses linearly.

Hybrid dispatch (M <= 8 -> tile_mma_v1, M > 8 -> marlin_gemm) is a deployable Stage 15.E win WITHOUT tensor cores. ~1.20x decode-side win at M = 1..8 maps directly to c=1 (1 token/forward) and c=4 multi_step=4 (M=4-8) workloads — exactly the 91 % AWQ-Marlin slice from the Stage 14-C decode profile.
implications_for_stage_15: Stage 15.E should NOT just gate on M >= 8 (the original plan); the gate that maximises wins is M < ~12, route to tile_mma_v1, otherwise route to existing marlin_gemm.

Stage 15.D-body (tensor-core mma.m16n8k16) is still the largest potential win — it would beat both the software path and the production dequant+matmul at M = 16..256 (the prefill / large-batch decode regime). But its priority shifts from 'unblock any win' to 'win the high-M regime'.

If the hybrid dispatch lands first (low risk), the Stage 14-C-projected c=8 lift drops a bit because the M = 32 case is unaffected by the low-M hybrid. But the c=1 / c=4 cases get a free 1.10-1.24x today.
Both wins can stack: hybrid dispatch + tensor-core MMA = best of both regimes.
